markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- In its normal form, a _spy staff_ is a slim rod of burnished wood with subtle etchings of eyes upon its sides. The first to develop the _spy staff_ were agents of Andoran's Twilight Talons, but such staves have spread to espionage agencies throughout the Inner Sea region and beyond. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Effect** You change the shape and appearance of this staff to that of an ordinary handheld accessory of your choosing of the same Bulk. The staff’s statistics don’t change. Only a creature benefiting from [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) or a similar effect can attempt to disbelieve this illusion, with a DC of 27. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Spy Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 340 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
  • **1st** [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [_invisible item_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2004), [_message rune_](/Spells.aspx?ID=581)
  • **2nd** [_disguise magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491), [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560), _illusory disguise_
Spy Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 1,175 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The DC to disbelieve the illusion disguising the staff is 32.
  • **3rd** [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), _illusory disguise_, [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
  • **4th** _clairvoyance_, [_peaceful bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1624)
Spy Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 5,300 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The DC to disbelieve the illusion disguising the staff is 38.
  • **5th** [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661)
  • **6th** [_mislead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605), [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662)
name: Spy Staff (Major)

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Carved from white ash wood, a staff of air crackles with electrical sparks, and a breeze always follows the wielder. While wielding a _staff of air_, you feel lighter on your feet, and you can Step into difficult terrain once per round. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Staff of Air **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 60 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_gale blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=917)
  • **1st** [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5), [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143)
Staff of Air (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 450 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_ash cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=865), [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210)
  • **3rd** [_blazing dive_](/Spells.aspx?ID=869), [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=367)
Staff of Air (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 134 **Price** 1,800 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **4th** [_ash cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=865), [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)
  • **5th** [_blazing dive_](/Spells.aspx?ID=869), [_lightning storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2007)
name: Staff of Air

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Geometric patterns are etched into the smooth brown and gray surface of a _staff of earth_, which makes a solid thud whenever tapped against the ground. While wielding a _staff of_ earth, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude saves and DC against effects that [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Staff of Earth **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 60 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=990)
  • **1st** [_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=708)
Staff of Earth (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 450 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_expeditious excavation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=574), [_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=708)
  • **3rd** [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [_shifting sand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=586)
Staff of Earth (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 1,800 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **4th** _expeditious excavation_, [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610), [_shape stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667)
  • **5th** [_blazing fissure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=870), [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
name: Staff of Earth

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A _staff of water_ is most often made of driftwood, sometimes lacquered blue. Carved versions often have a wave pattern. The staff smells of rain or brine. While wielding a _staff of water_, you have resistance 2 to fire. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Staff of Water **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 60 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_spout_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1002)
  • **1st** [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=53), [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=154)
Staff of Water (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 450 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The resistance is 5.
  • **2nd** [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756)
  • **3rd** [_aqueous orb_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443), [_wall of water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1028)
Staff of Water (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 1,800 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The resistance is 10.
  • **4th** [_crashing wave_](/Spells.aspx?ID=682), [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=155)
  • **5th** [_control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=51), [_geyser_](/Spells.aspx?ID=918)
name: Staff of Water

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 13,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A _zealot staff's_ color, iconography, and materials vary depending on the faith it's dedicated to. An [Iomedaean](/Deities.aspx?ID=9) staff might be forged of gold and shaped like an ornamental sword, while a [Lamashtan](/Deities.aspx?ID=11) one could instead be made of blackened iron depicting monstrous faces. Used as a weapon, the staff is a _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_. The staff represents vehement support of the deity to whom the staff is dedicated, punishing defiance. When you prepare this staff, if you don’t worship its deity, you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 until your next daily preparations. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You hit with a Strike using the staff; **Effect** A vicious blast of deific power explodes as you hit. You deal an additional 1d4 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per damage die of the staff to the target of your Strike. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. Add the cleric spells granted by your deity to the list for their rank and each higher rank. For example, a zealot staff of [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292) would add [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) at 1st through 7th rank, [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) at 3rd through 7th rank, and [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) at 4th through 7th rank.
  • **Cantrip** [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447)
  • **2nd** [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445)
  • **3rd** [_warding aggression_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1029)
  • **4th** [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499)
  • **5th** [_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), _divine wrath_
  • **6th** [_spiritual armament_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687), [_zealous conviction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1760)
  • **7th** _crisis of faith_, [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495)
**Craft Requirements** You worship the deity to which the staff is dedicated. Supply one casting of all listed spells, including your deity’s cleric spells.
name: Zealot Staff

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A _zombie staff_ is etched with the rotting visage of an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) grimacing in terror and dismay carved atop it. The staff’s [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) spells can summon only undead that have flesh and an Intelligence modifier of –4 or lower. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. If you cast _summon undead_, you can also cast [_protect companion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=975) on the resulting minion as a free action. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Zombie Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 330 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** _protect companion_
  • **1st** [_necromancer’s generosity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=955), _summon undead_
  • **2nd** [_final sacrifice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1992), _summon undead_
Zombie Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 1,200 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_bind undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449), _necromancer’s generosity_, _summon undead_
  • **4th** _final sacrifice_, _summon undead_, [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
Zombie Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 135 **Price** 5,600 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **5th** _necromancer’s generosity_, _summon undead_
  • **6th** _final sacrifice_, [_necrotize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=956), _summon undead_
name: Zombie Staff

  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18)

  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710), [_wave of despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)

While playing the bagpipes, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks made to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Bagpipes of Turmoil **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_deafness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=62), [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=168), [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279)
  • **3rd** [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [_shift blame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=997)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [_painful vibrations_](/Spells.aspx?ID=964)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710), [_wave of despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)
name: Bagpipes of Turmoil (Greater)

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This handheld snare drum is adorned with garish scenes of battle and triumph. When played, no matter what rhythm, it always gives the impression of a marching beat, invoking armies on the move. While playing the drums, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks and a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Drums of War **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
  • **1st** [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
Drums of War (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_false vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [_spiritual armament_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687)
  • **3rd** [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)
Drums of War (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus to Performance checks is +2.
  • **4th** [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [_tortoise and the hare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1021)
  • **5th** _dispelling globe_, [_quicken time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1187), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710)
name: Drums of War

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This lustrous lute has a polished body that changes to show whatever decorations or carvings you find most appealing, or which best reinforce the story of the song you're currently playing (as a free action). Its frets are inlaid with mother of pearl. With its mix of enchantment and illusion magic, it's favored by many traveling minstrels. While playing the lute, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Entertainer's Lute **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **Cantrip** [_infectious enthusiasm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=932)
  • **1st** [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356)
Entertainer's Lute (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp **Bulk** L ---
  • **2nd** [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_phantasmal treasure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2013)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567)
Entertainer's Lute (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp **Bulk** L --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_honeyed words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [_infectious melody_](/Spells.aspx?ID=933)
  • **5th** [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
name: Entertainer's Lute

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 136 **Price** 10,000 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This exquisite fiddle is perfectly carved and balanced to produce the purest sound while granting its player perfect balance and poise. It grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re a master in Performance, while playing it you also gain a +1 status bonus to Reflex saves and a +1 item bonus to Dexterity-based skill checks. If you’re legendary in Performance, the bonuses are +2. When you [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) with the fiddle, you can choose to create a harsh and discordant note. You critically fail the Performance check, shred the strings of the fiddle, and create an 8th-rank [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), with a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you instead of a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). The fiddle can’t be played again until the strings are replaced at a cost of 200 gp. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470)
  • **2nd** [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516)
  • **4th** [_honeyed words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558)
  • **5th** [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665)
  • **6th** _calm_, [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741)
  • **7th** _dominate_, [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595), [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726), _vibrant pattern_, [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2040)
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.
name: Fiddle of the Maestro

Each has jade pupils and a semiprecious stone as its iris. The eyes flutter open and closed when the flute is played, as if it held the eyes of many independent beings. While playing the flute, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. ---

This blackened wood wand has a smoldering tip, emitting a slight trail of steam.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast the wand's spell, but the mist prevents creatures from being able to breathe in its area. They must hold their breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) for the 2nd-rank wand or [_cinder swarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1350) for the 4th-rank wand.

>
[Wand of Chromatic Burst](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2273)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intricately carved quartz wand changes color, cycling through the colors of the rainbow.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_chromatic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=881) of the indicated rank. Additionally, the target can use the Chromatic Armor Burst action.

 **Chromatic Armor Burst** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Requirements** You're affected by _chromatic armor_ created by the _wand of chromatic burst_; **Effect** Choose one color of the _chromatic armor_ the wand created for you. The spell ends and light of that color flashes brightly in a 20-foot emanation. Creatures in the area take damage of the type associated with the color you chose, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your spell DC; the amount of damage depends on the wand's type. This action has the trait corresponding to the damage type you chose.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _chromatic armor_ of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Chromatic Burst](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2273)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intricately carved quartz wand changes color, cycling through the colors of the rainbow.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_chromatic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=881) of the indicated rank. Additionally, the target can use the Chromatic Armor Burst action.

 **Chromatic Armor Burst** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Requirements** You're affected by _chromatic armor_ created by the _wand of chromatic burst_; **Effect** Choose one color of the _chromatic armor_ the wand created for you. The spell ends and light of that color flashes brightly in a 20-foot emanation. Creatures in the area take damage of the type associated with the color you chose, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your spell DC; the amount of damage depends on the wand's type. This action has the trait corresponding to the damage type you chose.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _chromatic armor_ of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Contagious Frailty](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2275)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Cracks and healed fractures spiderweb the shaft of this bone wand, worsening each time the wand is used. The bone's worn epiphysis forms the wand's pommel, and black leather wraps around the handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_enfeeble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513). After you cast the spell, if the target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), it releases a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that doesn’t include itself. Each creature in that area must attempt a Fortitude save as if targeted by enfeeble, but gets an outcome one degree of success better than it rolled.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _enfeeble_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Contagious Frailty

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Crushing Leaps](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2276)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This supple, light wooden wand drifts to the ground like a feather or leaf when dropped, landing unharmed. A thin coil of metal wraps around the wand's handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_jump_](/Spells.aspx?ID=167), but can jump up to 60 feet. When you land you shatter the ground, making each creature in a 5-foot emanation [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn. In addition, the space you land in and all squares in the emanation become difficult terrain for 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of 1st-rank _jump_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Crushing Leaps

    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dazzling Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2277)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Solidified radiance comprises this slender, featureless wand. It sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet. After you Activate the wand, the light fades, so it only sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The wand returns to its original brightness each dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) of the indicated rank, dazzling your target with the beam’s intensity. A creature that takes damage from the spell is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for a number of rounds equal to the spell rank. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target is also blinded for as long as it’s dazzled from the spell. However, it can attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the blinded condition on a success (but remaining dazzled).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _holy light_ of the appropriate rank.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dazzling Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2277)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Solidified radiance comprises this slender, featureless wand. It sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet. After you Activate the wand, the light fades, so it only sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The wand returns to its original brightness each dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) of the indicated rank, dazzling your target with the beam’s intensity. A creature that takes damage from the spell is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for a number of rounds equal to the spell rank. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target is also blinded for as long as it’s dazzled from the spell. However, it can attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the blinded condition on a success (but remaining dazzled).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _holy light_ of the appropriate rank.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dumbfounding Doom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2278)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carvings of skulls, monsters, and all manner of violence decorate this wand of blackened bone, but it makes absurd sounds when Activated, such as a honking horn, a manic giggle, or a daydreamy sigh.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the indicated rank, showing the target a potential death that's gruesome and absurd. If the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by the spell, it also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) with a value 1 higher than the frightened value. This lasts for the duration of the spell.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _impending doom_ of the appropriate rank.

    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carvings of skulls, monsters, and all manner of violence decorate this wand of blackened bone, but it makes absurd sounds when Activated, such as a honking horn, a manic giggle, or a daydreamy sigh.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the indicated rank, showing the target a potential death that's gruesome and absurd. If the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) by the spell, it also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) with a value 1 higher than the frightened value. This lasts for the duration of the spell.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _impending doom_ of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Hawthorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2279)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from a hawthorn branch, this wand has a smooth handle, but the shaft remains covered in bark and long thorns. Polished red stones, arranged like a cluster of berries, decorate the pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617) of the indicated rank, and the target sprouts long thorns like those of a hawthorn tree. While _oaken resilience_ lasts, any creature that hits the target with an unarmed Strike or otherwise touches it takes piercing damage from the thorns, with the amount determined by the wand’s type. A creature that has [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) the target takes this damage as well at the start of each of the target’s turns.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _Oaken Resilience_ of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Hawthorn (2nd-Rank Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The thorns deal 1d4 piercing damage.

level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from a hawthorn branch, this wand has a smooth handle, but the shaft remains covered in bark and long thorns. Polished red stones, arranged like a cluster of berries, decorate the pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617) of the indicated rank, and the target sprouts long thorns like those of a hawthorn tree. While _oaken resilience_ lasts, any creature that hits the target with an unarmed Strike or otherwise touches it takes piercing damage from the thorns, with the amount determined by the wand’s type. A creature that has [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) the target takes this damage as well at the start of each of the target’s turns.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _Oaken Resilience_ of the appropriate rank.

</column>

<title

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Hybrid Form](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2280)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The grain of this simple wooden wand forms shifting images of sharp claws, snapping jaws, and countless creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; This activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, selecting two forms from among those you can normally choose. You gain the benefits of both forms. For example, if one form can breathe air and the other can breathe underwater, you can breathe in both situations. If there's overlap in abilities, you gain the better one. For instance, if both have a fly Speed, you get the higher one, and if both forms have claws, you gain only the claw Strike you prefer. The GM determines which abilities overlap and which are cumulative.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, must have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) trait, and must allow more than one choice of battle form.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The grain of this simple wooden wand forms shifting images of sharp claws, snapping jaws, and countless creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; This activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, selecting two forms from among those you can normally choose. You gain the benefits of both forms. For example, if one form can breathe air and the other can breathe underwater, you can breathe in both situations. If there's overlap in abilities, you gain the better one. For instance, if both have a fly Speed, you get the higher one, and if both forms have claws, you gain only the claw Strike you prefer. The GM determines which abilities overlap and which are cumulative.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, must have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) trait, and must allow more than one choice of battle form.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Hybrid Form](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2280)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The grain of this simple wooden wand forms shifting images of sharp claws, snapping jaws, and countless creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; This activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, selecting two forms from among those you can normally choose. You gain the benefits of both forms. For example, if one form can breathe air and the other can breathe underwater, you can breathe in both situations. If there's overlap in abilities, you gain the better one. For instance, if both have a fly Speed, you get the higher one, and if both forms have claws, you gain only the claw Strike you prefer. The GM determines which abilities overlap and which are cumulative.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, must have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) trait, and must allow more than one choice of battle form.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Legerdemain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2281)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand of lacquered black wood has a handle wrapped in interwoven colorful ribbons. A silver bell caps the wand's tasseled pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163))   **Requirements** The last action you took this turn was to Cast a Spell from the wand; **Effect** You make yourself the center of attention. An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a hostile act, or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against it.

 The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check, or Dismiss the activation.

 You can Sustain this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Legerdemain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2281)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand of lacquered black wood has a handle wrapped in interwoven colorful ribbons. A silver bell caps the wand's tasseled pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163))   **Requirements** The last action you took this turn was to Cast a Spell from the wand; **Effect** You make yourself the center of attention. An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a hostile act, or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against it.

 The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check, or Dismiss the activation.

 You can Sustain this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank.

An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a hostile act, or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check against it.

 The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check, or Dismiss the activation.

 You can Sustain this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) of the indicated rank, and can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect on the same target. Treat the _soothe_ spell's level as 1 higher for this counteract check.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _soothe_ of the appropriate rank.

**Bulk** L

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Red feathers hang from the handle of this ivory wand. Holding it brings a sense of gentle calm.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) of the indicated rank, and can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect on the same target. Treat the _soothe_ spell's level as 1 higher for this counteract check.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _soothe_ of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Mercy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2283)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pommel of this rose quartz wand resembles the stylized wings of an angel. When you cast its spell and choose not to make it [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), the crystal deepens to blood red. The color reverts to rose when you cast the spell from the wand nonlethally.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) traits.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pommel of this rose quartz wand resembles the stylized wings of an angel. When you cast its spell and choose not to make it [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), the crystal deepens to blood red. The color reverts to rose when you cast the spell from the wand nonlethally.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) traits.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) traits.

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This greasy stick emits a stomach-churning scent when held in hand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) of the indicated rank. A creature that takes initial acid damage from this spell become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. Use your spell DC if the creature attempts to recover from this sickness. This is an [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) effect.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _acid grip_ of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Paralytic Shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2285)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A two-pronged metal rod, this wand has a handle coated in thick rubber. Each prong ends in a copper coil. When Activated, the wand produces a loud zapping sound.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), electrocuting the target into immobility. Each target takes electricity damage at the start of its turns while it remains [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) due to the spell. The amount of damage depends on the wand's type.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _paralyze_ of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Paralytic Shock (3rd-Rank Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 141

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The electrocution deals 1d12 electricity damage.

level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Paralytic Shock (7th-Rank Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 141

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The electrocution deals 2d12 electricity damage.

---

This wand is made of chitin, topped with a hooked barb that weeps droplets of foul-smelling, green fluid when you Activate the wand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast a Spell determined by the wand's type. The poison the spell delivers gains the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _spider sting_ of the appropriate rank.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; This activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell. Its range increases by 30 feet. As normal for increasing ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, its range extends to 30 feet.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate rank. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, and must have a range.

>
[Wand of Refracting Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2288)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is short and wide with a hexagonal, crystal shaft and a leather-wrapped handle. The wand refracts direct bright light into a rainbow.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_chromatic ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=883) of the indicated rank. After you cast the spell, if you hit the target, the light refracts to another creature within 30 feet of the first target. Roll your spell attack roll and to determine the ray's color separately for each target. The ray continues to refract each time it hits. The refraction ceases if you miss any target, and you can end the refraction at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _chromatic ray_ of the appropriate rank.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Rolling Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2289)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_floating flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533) of the indicated rank. If you create the flame on the ground, the ground in the sphere’s square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand’s type. If a creature in the flames doesn’t move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it’s in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it), provided it’s on the ground.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _floating flame_ of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Rolling Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2289)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_floating flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533) of the indicated rank. If you create the flame on the ground, the ground in the sphere’s square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand’s type. If a creature in the flames doesn’t move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it’s in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it), provided it’s on the ground.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _floating flame_ of the appropriate rank.

</column>

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_floating flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533) of the indicated rank. If you create the flame on the ground, the ground in the sphere’s square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand’s type. If a creature in the flames doesn’t move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it’s in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it), provided it’s on the ground.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _floating flame_ of the appropriate rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Shocking Haze](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2290)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Thin, shiny wires crisscross the surface of this smooth, black wand. The pommel is capped with a polished metal sphere, which sometimes emits small electric sparks.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), and the wand creates faint orbs of electricity [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) in the haze around you. Each time a creature that’s adjacent to you fails a flat check against the concealment from _blur_, they explode one of the orbs, causing them to take 5 electricity damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _blur_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Shocking Haze

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Teeming Ghosts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142, [The Slithering](/Sources.aspx?ID=41) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale wooden wand is carved to resemble a thigh bone with metal caps at each end. Ghostly tendrils seem to swirl around it every so often.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_false vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523) of the indicated rank. During the duration of the spell, wisps that resemble spirits flit around you as long as you have any temporary Hit Points from _false vitality_, and you can use the following action.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You successfully impart the [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition on a creature; **Requirements** You have at least 1 temporary Hit Point from _false vitality_; **Effect** You end _false vitality_ and increase the creature's frightened condition value to 2.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _false vitality_ of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Teeming Ghosts (2nd-Rank Spell)
</title>

<traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Teeming Ghosts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 142, [The Slithering](/Sources.aspx?ID=41) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale wooden wand is carved to resemble a thigh bone with metal caps at each end. Ghostly tendrils seem to swirl around it every so often.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_false vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523) of the indicated rank. During the duration of the spell, wisps that resemble spirits flit around you as long as you have any temporary Hit Points from _false vitality_, and you can use the following action.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You successfully impart the [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition on a creature; **Requirements** You have at least 1 temporary Hit Point from _false vitality_; **Effect** You end _false vitality_ and increase the creature's frightened condition value to 2.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _false vitality_ of the appropriate rank.

    right="Item 6+"

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale wooden wand is carved to resemble a thigh bone with metal caps at each end. Ghostly tendrils seem to swirl around it every so often.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_false vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523) of the indicated rank. During the duration of the spell, wisps that resemble spirits flit around you as long as you have any temporary Hit Points from _false vitality_, and you can use the following action.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You successfully impart the [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition on a creature; **Requirements** You have at least 1 temporary Hit Point from _false vitality_; **Effect** You end _false vitality_ and increase the creature's frightened condition value to 2.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _false vitality_ of the appropriate rank.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of the Ash Puppet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2293)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 143

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is composed of ash that has been compressed, shaped, and sealed with a clear lacquer. When you trace the wand's tip along a solid surface, it leaves a black trail of charcoal. Writing with the wand in this way never damages or wears the wand down.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast _disintegrate_. If the spell reduces a living creature to fine powder, you animate that creature's ashes into a [sulfur zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1377) with the same general appearance as the disintegrated creature. You control this sulfur zombie, which gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) and [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) traits. You can issue a verbal command to the sulfur zombie as a single action with the auditory and concentrate traits. The sulfur zombie crumbles into inanimate ash when reduced to 0 Hit Points or after 1 minute, whichever comes first.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _disintegrate_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of the Ash Puppet

    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 143

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This extravagant wand is made of gold and capped with a large, sparkling gemstone. Its handle is wrapped in plush, padded fabric.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_pet cache_](/Spells.aspx?ID=706) but the accommodations inside the extradimensional space are luxurious and spacious. The food is delicious gourmet cuisine tailored to the pet's palate, the habitat is the perfect temperature and environment for the pet, complete with comfortable bed or lounging area. A pair of phantom hands pamper the pet, patting, grooming, or playing with it at the creature's whim.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _pet cache_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Wand of Tormented Slumber](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2295)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Sleep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=145" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 143

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The carved talon of an unidentifiable beast comprises this wand. Blood-stained cloth wraps the thicker part of the talon, which acts as a handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), but the slumbering creatures have terrifying nightmares. A creature knocked [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) by this spell takes 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). This damage wakes the creature from unconsciousness only if it deals 4 or more damage on a single roll. If the creature awakens from its unconsciousness due to damage (whether it was the persistent mental damage or not), it's [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). If it awakens from damage on its own turn, the creature doesn't reduce its frightened condition automatically on that turn.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of 4th-rank _sleep_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Tormented Slumber

When you hold this sleek, shiny gray wand, you hear a faint chorus of overlapping whispers.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=193). The first time the target rolls a success at a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to mislead your probe, it gets a result one step worse than it rolled. This means you learn the answer if the target's Deception check would have succeeded, and you learn nothing rather than believing a falsehood if the check would have been a critical success.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _mind probe_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Catching](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2299)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This rune was not reprinted in the remastered Treasure Vault book. _

A _catching_ rune creates a small, magical vacuum that attempts to pull weapons away from opponents. If you can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) as part of a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839), such as with the [Disarming Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=1896) feat or this rune's activated ability, a catching shield grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to such Disarm attempts.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You Shield Block a melee Strike made with a held weapon;  **Effect** You attempt to Disarm the creature whose attack you blocked of the weapon they attacked you with. You can do so even if you don't have a hand free.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Catching

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Menacing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2300)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied any visible article of clothing

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Common among brutes who use the magic to scare others into compliance, _menacing_ runes lend you a formidable appearance, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) others.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Menacing
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Menacing (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This rune grants a +2 item bonus and has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The rune casts a 3rd-rank [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) spell (DC 25).
name: Menacing

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pontoon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2301)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to footwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Footwear with _pontoon_ runes allows you to traverse waterways with ease. While wearing footwear with the rune, you can walk on the surface of water and other liquids without falling through. This offers no protection against damage the liquid deals. You can go under the liquid's surface, but you must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) if you do.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pontoon

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Preserving](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2302)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a basket, bag, or other container

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Preserving_ runes are common among merchants and other travelers who are on the road for weeks or months at a time. Any non-magical food and drink inside a container with a _preserving_ rune remains fresher for longer, taking ten times as long to spoil. This feature doesn't prolong the duration of alchemical items.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Preserving
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Preserving (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 144

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Non-magical food and drink inside the container never spoil, and the rune has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The container casts [_cleanse cuisine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468) on all the food and drink within.
name: Preserving

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Retaliation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2303)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This rune was not reprinted in the remastered Treasure Vault book. _

Shields with a _retaliation_ rune use the impact of weapons to generate blasts of explosive force that counterattack foes.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) a melee Strike;  **Effect** You unleash force damage against the creature that made the triggering attack. This attack deals 4d4 force damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retaliation (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retaliation (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage is 8d4 force damage and the save DC is 27.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retaliation (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage is 12d4 force damage and the save DC is 31.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Retaliation (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage is 16d4 force damage and the save DC is 41.
name: Retaliation

_Trackless runes_ are common among hunters and trappers, as well as thieves and anyone fleeing pursuit. While wearing trackless footwear, you are continuously affected by the [_vanishing tracks_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737) spell.

**Price** 900 gp

</row>

</column>

---

The rune has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You extend the effect of your rune out to a 20-foot emanation. The emanation remains for 8 hours, affecting up to 10 creatures of your choice within the area. You can Dismiss this effect.
