markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemist's Haversack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2305)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _alchemist’s haversack_ is a sturdy leather backpack with two compartments. The main section contains an extradimensional space equivalent to a [spacious pouch type II](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032), perfect for carrying bulkier alchemist equipment. A secondary partition can hold 2 Bulk of items, 1 of which doesn’t count against your Bulk limit. This second compartment can also be activated (see below).

Two shoulder straps and a thick belt for the waist hold the haversack on the wearer securely. These straps have pouches and loops for keeping alchemical items handy. They also contain magically [expanded alchemist’s toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702) that grants you a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to create alchemical items.

Water and undesirable material can’t seep into the haversack, which cleans itself once per hour. When you reach in any part of the haversack, the item you sought is the first one you find. Also, the haversack preserves mundane ingredients, food, and drink inside, so they stay fresh indefinitely. This feature doesn’t prolong the duration of magic or alchemical items.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You can create versatile vials; **Effect** You pull a versatile vial from the satchel’s secondary compartment. It’s as effective as the vials you create at the start of the day, not limited like ones created using [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2801).

**Craft Requirements** You are an [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=1).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alchemist's Haversack

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Everyneed Pack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2306)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 85

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this item_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Constructed of green material and decorated with a white Glyph of the Open Road, an _everyneed pack_ has a dozen or more small pockets lining the inside. The pack is enchanted so that each pocket contains common, mundane gear, each item worth no more than 1 gp, such as [chalk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=12), [flint and steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=21), and string. It doesn't contain any armor, shields, weapons, or items made of precious material. Keep track of the exact value of the gear you retrieve from the pack. The pack becomes a mundane [backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4) after items of your choice with a combined value of 8 gp have been removed from it.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 1 gp or less.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Everyneed Pack
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 85

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Everyneed Pack (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 85

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

A pocket contains mundane gear worth no more than 5 gp each. When you activate the pack, you can draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 5 gp or less, and the pack becomes a mundane backpack after 45 gp worth of items are removed.
name: Everyneed Pack

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this item_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Constructed of green material and decorated with a white Glyph of the Open Road, an _everyneed pack_ has a dozen or more small pockets lining the inside. The pack is enchanted so that each pocket contains common, mundane gear, each item worth no more than 1 gp, such as [chalk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=12), [flint and steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=21), and string. It doesn't contain any armor, shields, weapons, or items made of precious material. Keep track of the exact value of the gear you retrieve from the pack. The pack becomes a mundane [backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4) after items of your choice with a combined value of 8 gp have been removed from it.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 1 gp or less.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Bulk** 1

</row>

---

A pocket contains mundane gear worth no more than 5 gp each. When you activate the pack, you can draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 5 gp or less, and the pack becomes a mundane backpack after 45 gp worth of items are removed.
name: Everyneed Pack (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vaultbreaker's Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2307)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [Pathfinder #158: Sixty Feet Under](/Sources.aspx?ID=47) pg. 76

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _vaultbreaker's harness_ has four pockets across the chest. The pockets contain a set of [infiltrator's thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58), infiltrator picks, a [levered crowbar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=18), and a [glass cutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=870). These items are magically bound to the harness; if they are more than 1 foot away from you, they disappear, then reappear in the harness at the next sunset. [Broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) or destroyed items similarly reappear, restored, in their proper pockets at sunset.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You cinch the harness to prepare for mischief. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and a +10-foot item bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaultbreaker's Harness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bellflower Toolbelt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2308)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145, [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Different versions of the _bellflower toolbelt_ are customized to appear to suit specific trades, so a belt used for carpentry would look different from a belt for baking.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You place an object of up to 1 Bulk into the belt, transforming that object into a tool befitting the trade for which the belt was created. Each object remains transformed until it has been removed from the belt for 24 hours or someone uses a single Interact action to return it to its normal form. If enough transformed items are in it, the belt can be used as [artisan's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3) for that trade.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bellflower Toolbelt

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bravery Baldric](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2309)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bravery baldric_ is a belt that wraps around the shoulder and draws on your well of courage. When you critically succeed on a save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect or reduce your [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition to 0, the baldric gains 1 charge, which slightly alters the color. A _bravery baldric_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. You can have only one _bravery baldric_ invested at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** The baldric has a charge; **Effect** One charge in the baldric expires, and you gain its benefit, according to its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravery Baldric (Fleet)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The baldric is grass green when charged, and it casts [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531) on you.

level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The baldric is light blue when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=125). 

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravery Baldric (Haste)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 145

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The baldric is silver when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=147). 

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravery Baldric (Healthful)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

The baldric is golden when charged, and it casts a 2nd-rank [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) on you.

As the _healthful baldric_, but it casts a 4th-rank _sound body_.

The baldric is dark gray when charged, and it casts [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) on you.

A _bravery baldric_ is a belt that wraps around the shoulder and draws on your well of courage. When you critically succeed on a save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect or reduce your [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition to 0, the baldric gains 1 charge, which slightly alters the color. A _bravery baldric_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. You can have only one _bravery baldric_ invested at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** The baldric has a charge; **Effect** One charge in the baldric expires, and you gain its benefit, according to its type.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clockwork Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2310)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146, [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 79

**Price** 20,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Paper-thin interlocking cogs and gears make up the bronze _clockwork cloak_.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're struck by a melee attack with a held weapon;  **Effect** The folds of the cloak attempt to divert the attack and catch the weapon in the cloak's gears. Make an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) the attacking creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You wrap the cloak around yourself and the winding gears decelerate your body, causing you to enter standby mode. While in standby mode you don't need to eat, drink, or sleep. You remain aware of your surroundings but take a –4 penalty to Perception checks. You can stay in standby mode indefinitely, although your body ages normally. You can leave standby mode as a free action. If you do so to initiate combat, you gain a +2 item bonus to your initiative roll.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Clockwork Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sure-Step Crampons](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2312)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146, [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 81

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sure-step crampons are sturdy leather boots with warm fur lining and magically augmented steel crampons that offer the wearer better purchase on ice. They allow you to walk across ice without difficulty, ignoring the uneven ground and difficult terrain caused by ice, and reducing greater difficult terrain caused by ice to difficult terrain.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** You're standing on an earthen, icy, or wooden surface; **Effect** You dig the crampons into the spot where you're standing, offering additional support until the next time you move. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to [move you](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The bonus lasts until you move from your current spot.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sure-Step Crampons
